evals.name: node-agent-tools
evals.prompt: My AI agent makes tool calls and I need to rate limit per user and block prompt injection before each tool runs.
evals.expected_output: Modifies agent.ts to add @arcjet/guard imports, creates guard client at module scope, configures tokenBucket and detectPromptInjection rules, calls guard() inline in each tool function with a specific label, handles DENY decisions.
assertions: Uses @arcjet/guard package (not @arcjet/node or @arcjet/next)
Creates guard client with launchArcjet() at module scope in agent.ts
Configures a rate limiting rule (tokenBucket, fixedWindow, or slidingWindow)
Configures detectPromptInjection() rule
Calls guard() with a label parameter
Passes user identifier as rate limit key
Checks decision.conclusion for DENY
Calls guard() inline per tool (not via shared wrapper)
Does not use HTTP SDK (@arcjet/node or @arcjet/next)

evals.name: python-agent-tools
evals.expected_output: Modifies agent.py to add arcjet.guard imports, creates async guard client with launch_arcjet() at module scope, configures TokenBucket, DetectPromptInjection, and LocalDetectSensitiveInfo rules, calls guard() inline in each tool function or per-tool in the handler, handles DENY decisions.
assertions: Adds arcjet to requirements.txt
Imports from arcjet.guard module
Creates async guard client with launch_arcjet() (not launch_arcjet_sync, since agent.py uses async code)
Configures a rate limiting rule (TokenBucket, FixedWindow, or SlidingWindow)
Configures DetectPromptInjection() rule
Calls guard() in agent.py
Checks decision.conclusion for DENY
Passes user identifier as rate limit key
Calls guard() inline per tool (not via single shared wrapper for all tools)
Does not use HTTP SDK (arcjet() / arcjet_sync() / protect())

evals.name: node-mcp-tool
evals.prompt: Make sure my mcp server is secure
evals.expected_output: Modifies server.ts to add @arcjet/guard imports, creates guard client at module scope, configures rate limiting and detectPromptInjection rules, calls guard() inline in each tool handler with a descriptive label, handles DENY decisions.
assertions: Uses @arcjet/guard package
Creates guard client with launchArcjet()
Configures a rate limiting rule
Configures detectPromptInjection() rule
Calls guard() with a label parameter
Calls guard() inline in each tool handler (not via shared wrapper)
Checks decision.conclusion for DENY
Does not use HTTP SDK (@arcjet/node or @arcjet/next)

evals.name: go-agent
evals.expected_output: Explains that Arcjet Guard is only available for JavaScript/TypeScript and Python, not Go. Does not hallucinate a Go Arcjet package or create a hand-rolled imitation.
assertions: Does not hallucinate a Go Arcjet package
Does not add @arcjet/guard to a Go project
Does not create a hand-rolled guard implementation
Communicates that Guard is not available for Go
